text:
Once upon a time in a small village nestled between rolling hills, there lived a young girl named Alice. Alice adored singing folk songs that told tales of adventure and fairies. Every evening after school, she would slip out of bed and gather her butterfly nets to fly around her village serenading birds and listening to stories from old people who knew long forgotten songs.

One evening, after completing her nightly bird tour, Alice decided to head home. The stars shone brightly, promising a clear night. As she floated along, singing about a magical kingdom far, far away, she noticed a peculiar glow emanating from an old, abandoned barn.

Her curiosity piqued, she flew closer. Inside stood an ancient book resting atop a pillow. It was a book she had heard about from a wandering bard: "The Chronicles of Glitterland." Curiously, she read a few lines aloud, thinking the stars heard her imaginatively.
